design_philosophy.core_loop: One-on-one fighting tournament combat with simplified 3-button input system (punch, kick, throw) using hold-to-strengthen mechanic similar to original Street Fighter
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Learning opponent patterns and managing attack strength levels within a stripped-down control scheme competing against arcade-style fighting game complexity
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Battle historical and fictional 'World Heroes' throughout time via scientist Dr. Brown's time machine tournament
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately simplified control philosophy (3 buttons instead of 6) and unique Death Match mode with arena hazards (spikes, mines, burning ropes, slick floors) that dynamically alter combat conditions; emphasis on accessibility over button complexity while maintaining depth through strength-level management

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Tournament mode vs Death Match mode selection provides difficulty modulation; SNES version allows custom button assignment for 4 basic attacks, giving players control over input preference

progression.unlock_mechanism: Tournament bracket advancement; character roster includes both historical figures and fictional fighters unlocked through progression
replayability_hooks: Death Match mode with randomized hazard arrangements
Multiple character roster with distinct playstyles
Custom button assignment options in SNES version
Historical/fictional character premise encourages multiple playthroughs to experience different fighter narratives

world_building_techniques: Time machine tournament premise frames all combat
Character roster mixing historical figures (implied) with fictional fighters creates temporal collision narrative
Stage/arena design contextualizes battles within the tournament framework

faction_indicators: Regional censorship of Brocken character (Nazi imagery removal in European version reflects geopolitical sensitivity)
danger_signals: Death Match mode's hazard mechanics (spiked walls, landmines, fire) normalize violence escalation
Potential historical figure appropriation in character roster
environmental_storytelling: Death Match arenas filled with traps and hazards communicate danger/spectacle
Character costume design reflects their historical/fictional background

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - World Heroes (SNES)
